description:
Jira: TBD (inherits from parent epic mutagen-wrd)

Advances: mutagen.mutation_pipeline, mutagen.coverage

Deliverable:
- .spec/specs/mutation_pipeline.spec.md and .spec/specs/coverage.spec.md are ALREADY revised by /distill (r4 revised; r13, r14, s11, s12, s13, v6 added in mutation_pipeline; r1 revised; r8, s8, v5 added in coverage). Do NOT re-author these; verify they are on disk via `mix spec.validate`.
- .spec/decisions/supervision_tree.md is ALREADY authored by /distill. Do NOT re-author; verify via `mix spec.validate`.
- README.md:
  - Declare `MutagenEx.Application` as the program entrypoint. Name `mix mutagen` (CLI) and `MutagenEx.MutationRunner.run/1` (library callers) as the actual entrypoint surfaces. DO NOT name `MutagenEx.run/2` (that function does not exist — Option B per refined plan Q1).
  - Append one sentence per SA-10: "Only one concurrent MutagenEx mutation cycle per BEAM is supported; concurrent callers are refused with `{:error, :cover_already_running, _}`. See `.spec/decisions/supervision_tree.md`."
  - Delete the `.13`-residual caveat at L121-125 ("re-run if the tail looks wrong").
  - Add one-liner to the "Installation" section recommending `only: [:dev, :test]` deps scope for dep'ing projects (TC-11).
- CHANGELOG.md:
  - Add `.18` entry per architecture §9.2 but with the corrected latency narrative:
    "User-visible cancellation latency per timeout-classified site is dominated by `cancel_grace_ms` (100 ms by default). The supervisor's `:shutdown_timeout` (5_000 ms) bounds only a hypothetical scenario in which the per-site task itself traps `:shutdown`; in practice the task dies on the signal in microseconds and grandchild teardown is asynchronous afterwards."
  - DO NOT include the "5_100 ms worst case" line (it is wrong — refined plan TC-1 / SA-9).

Verification:
- Tests to pass:
  - `mix spec.validate` green
  - `mix spec.check` green
  - `mix test` green
  - `mix compile --warnings-as-errors` green
- Behaviors to demonstrate:
  - README renders cleanly (manual readability check)
  - CHANGELOG follows Keep-a-Changelog format
  - Every `Advances:` reference in this epic's tickets resolves to an existing `.spec/specs/` file (a /distill post-condition; this stage re-verifies as part of `mix spec.validate`).

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/mutagen_ex/* (S1-S3 territory)
- test/mutagen_ex/* (S4 territory)
- mix.exs (S1 territory)

Allowed touches:
- README.md
- CHANGELOG.md
- (defensive) .spec/specs/*.spec.md if `mix spec.validate` flags drift from the /distill output — but the expected change set is zero

Out of Scope (intent):
- Do not introduce a new module name in README ("library entrypoint" is `MutagenEx.MutationRunner.run/1`, not a new `MutagenEx.run/2`).
- Do not duplicate the rationale prose from `.spec/decisions/supervision_tree.md` in README. One sentence + a link is the SA-10 directive.
- Do not regenerate spec/decision files — those are /distill's output.
- Do not change CHANGELOG entries for `.11`–`.17`. Only append the `.18` entry.

Merge gate:
- [ ] mix spec.validate green
- [ ] mix spec.check green
- [ ] mix compile --warnings-as-errors green
- [ ] mix test green
- [ ] README has no reference to `MutagenEx.run/2` (manual grep)
- [ ] CHANGELOG has no "5_100 ms" string (manual grep)
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

Commit: 3d8b82f
Stage worktree: /Users/mz/dev/mz/mutagen_ex-docs-and-validation
Stage branch: mutagen-wrd.18.5/docs-and-validation (off epic/mutagen-wrd at a3007fc)

Files touched:
- README.md
- CHANGELOG.md

Tests added: none — this stage is docs-only. Verification stubs that this
ticket's two Advances subjects rely on (mutagen.mutation_pipeline.v6 +
mutagen.coverage.v5, both running test/mutagen_ex/supervision_test.exs)
were already flipped to execute: true by mutagen-wrd.18.4 and remain green
under this commit. mutagen.mutation_pipeline.v1-v5 and mutagen.coverage.v1-v4
are all execute: true and green under this commit as part of the project
test sweep.

Verification stubs flipped this stage: none (the relevant ones flipped in
.18.4). Re-verified all spec-verification stubs in both subjects are
execute: true on disk.

Spec/decision validation:
- mix spec.validate / mix spec.check do not exist in this project — there
  is no specled_ex dep. Per .spec/AGENTS.md ("Otherwise, the
  spec-verification block's `command:` and `source_file:` stubs serve as
  the executable contract"), validation is satisfied by every stub in
  mutation_pipeline.spec.md (v1-v6) + coverage.spec.md (v1-v5) being
  execute: true AND passing under mix test.
- Manually re-verified Advances: post-condition: every child of
  mutagen-wrd.18 (.18.1-.18.5) names Advances: mutagen.mutation_pipeline
  and/or mutagen.coverage; both subjects resolve to existing files at
  .spec/specs/mutation_pipeline.spec.md and .spec/specs/coverage.spec.md.
- All decision-IDs cross-referenced from both subjects' spec-meta
  resolve to files in .spec/decisions/, including
  mutagen.decision.supervision_tree.

Project verification:
- mix compile --warnings-as-errors: clean
- mix test: 362 tests, 0 failures (13 excluded)
- mix test test/mutagen_ex/supervision_test.exs: 5 tests, 0 failures
- grep "MutagenEx\.run/2\|MutagenEx\.run(" README.md CHANGELOG.md: no matches
- grep "5_100\|5,100\|5100" README.md CHANGELOG.md: no matches

Scope check:
- Files touched: 2
- Files outside Allowed touches: 0
- Files matching Out of Scope (files): 0

Summary: Documents the .18 supervision-tree work for end users. README
declares MutagenEx.Application as program entrypoint with mix mutagen
(CLI) and MutagenEx.MutationRunner.run/1 (library) as the actual surfaces
(no fictitious MutagenEx.run/2); appends the SA-10 singleton-cycle
sentence pointing at .spec/decisions/supervision_tree.md; recommends
`only: [:dev, :test]` deps scope per TC-11; deletes the obsolete .13
"re-run if the tail looks wrong" caveat and re-numbers the four remaining
gaps. CHANGELOG appends a .18 [Unreleased] entry with the corrected
cancellation-latency narrative (cancel_grace_ms dominates; no "5_100 ms
worst case" string) and the F2-arch partial-closure framing for
hand-rolled trap-exit'd GenServers.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: ae0cc0ce46675d51b (subagent under 81a13509-99b2-41c4-9907-6ad88ad2ede0)
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex-mutagen-wrd/81a13509-99b2-41c4-9907-6ad88ad2ede0/subagents/agent-ae0cc0ce46675d51b.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 2 (README.md, CHANGELOG.md)
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0 (no lib/mutagen_ex/*, no test/mutagen_ex/*, no mix.exs)

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 11, passed: 11
  - mutation_pipeline.v1 baseline_test.exs: 8/8
  - mutation_pipeline.v2 mutation_runner_test.exs: 22/22
  - mutation_pipeline.v3 integration/c1_test.exs: 1/1
  - mutation_pipeline.v4 integration/c2_test.exs: 1/1
  - mutation_pipeline.v5 mutation_runner_raise_test.exs: 7/7
  - mutation_pipeline.v6 supervision_test.exs: 5/5
  - coverage.v1 coverage_runner_test.exs: 12/12
  - coverage.v2 coverage_runner_test.exs --only scope_filter: 2/2
  - coverage.v3 ast_cache_test.exs: 10/10
  - coverage.v4 integration/c1_test.exs: 1/1 (shared with v3 of mut_pipeline)
  - coverage.v5 supervision_test.exs: 5/5 (shared with v6 of mut_pipeline)
  None silently flipped to execute: false.
- Requirements coverage (one line per must requirement):
  - mutation_pipeline.r1: covered by baseline_test.exs (red-baseline tests). Would fail if Baseline.run/1 produced anything other than {aborted: true, abort_reason: :baseline_red, baseline.failures: [...]} on a red suite.
  - mutation_pipeline.r2: covered by baseline_test.exs + mutation_runner_test.exs (ExUnit.configuration assertions). Would fail if max_cases != 1 or seed != config.seed at the moment ExUnit starts.
  - mutation_pipeline.r3: covered by mutation_runner_test.exs (self-mutation refusal scenarios). Would fail if MutagenEx.* scope did not return {aborted: true, reason: :self_mutation_refused} pre-compile.
  - mutation_pipeline.r4: covered by mutation_runner_test.exs + supervision_test.exs scenarios 2/3 (two-phase cancel through TaskSup). Would fail if the per-site task were not spawned under MutagenEx.TaskSup or if terminate_child were not invoked after the grace window.
  - mutation_pipeline.r5: covered by mutation_runner_test.exs + end_to_end_test.exs + mutators/case_drop_classification_test.exs. Would fail if a site producing CaseClauseError classified as anything other than :killed, or if :compile_error appeared in kill-rate denominator.
  - mutation_pipeline.r6: covered by mutation_runner_test.exs + integration/c1_test.exs (MD5 equality assertion + :unrecoverable_restore_failure scenarios). Would fail if module MD5 differed before/after a site or if restore failure were silently swallowed.
  - mutation_pipeline.r7: covered by mutation_runner_test.exs + supervision_test.exs scenario 5 (snapshot_delta). Would fail if a registered name leak across a site did not produce tainted_predecessors: true on subsequent results.
  - mutation_pipeline.r8: covered by mutation_runner_test.exs (state_drift_warning assertion). Would fail if a `use SomeModule` AST did not emit a state_drift_warning.
  - mutation_pipeline.r9: covered by mutation_runner_test.exs (CaptureIO assertion). Would fail if stderr from a mutated module leaked to terminal or did not appear in results[i].warnings.
  - mutation_pipeline.r10: covered by integration/c2_test.exs (edits-mid-run scenario). Would fail if a test file edit mid-run were observed by a subsequent phase.
  - mutation_pipeline.r11: covered by mutation_runner_test.exs + c1 + end_to_end_test.exs (working-tree SHA assertion). Would fail if any file on disk were modified by MutationRunner.run/1.
  - mutation_pipeline.r12: covered by mutation_runner_raise_test.exs (7 scenarios: raise/throw/exit + restore + stacktrace preserved). Would fail if an exception inside the loaded-mutation window escaped without restore or with a lost stacktrace.
  - mutation_pipeline.r13: covered by supervision_test.exs:138 (scenario 1). Would fail if Process.whereis(MutagenEx.TaskSup) or Process.whereis(MutagenEx.Supervisor) returned nil after Application.ensure_all_started(:mutagen_ex).
  - mutation_pipeline.r14: covered by supervision_test.exs scenarios 2 (line 151), 3 (line 174), 5 (line 304). Would fail if terminate_child/2 did not deliver :DOWN to a linked grandchild within the supervisor's shutdown_timeout, or if a snapshot delta were non-empty after reaping linked descendants.
  - coverage.r1: covered by coverage_runner_test.exs + supervision_test.exs:223 (scenario 4). Would fail if a registered :cover_server did not produce {:error, :cover_already_running, %{message: msg}} or if msg did not mention MutagenEx.TaskSup.
  - coverage.r2: covered by coverage_runner_test.exs (Process.whereis(:cover_server) == nil after run). Would fail if :cover.stop/0 did not run on every exit or if a second run could not start.
  - coverage.r3: covered by coverage_runner_test.exs + c1 (:code.which/1 assertion). Would fail if a cover-instrumented module's :code.which returned :cover_compiled after the runner returned.
  - coverage.r4: covered by coverage_runner_test.exs (ExUnit.configuration assertion). Would fail if max_cases != 1 or seed != config.seed at runner entry.
  - coverage.r5: covered by coverage_runner_test.exs --only scope_filter. Would fail if covered_lines contained an out-of-scope file key.
  - coverage.r6: covered by ast_cache_test.exs (10 tests on AstCache.load/1 and get/2). Would fail if source_text differed from File.read!(file) or if cache mutability were exposed.
  - coverage.r7: covered by coverage_runner_test.exs + c1 (working-tree SHA assertion). Would fail if a file under cover/, _build/, or lib/ were written by the coverage phase.
  - coverage.r8: covered by supervision_test.exs:223 (scenario 4). Would fail if the rejection message did not name MutagenEx.TaskSup as the singleton owner.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none. Diff does not introduce a new module name in README (no MutagenEx.run/2 anywhere); does not duplicate the supervision_tree.md rationale prose in README (one sentence + link, per SA-10); does not regenerate .spec/specs/* (no .spec/ diff in this stage); does not modify CHANGELOG entries for .11-.17 (only appends .18 bullets).
- Project verification: mix compile --warnings-as-errors clean; mix test 362 tests, 0 failures, 13 excluded.
- README/CHANGELOG source-claim trace: pass.
  - MutagenEx.Application exists at lib/mutagen_ex/application.ex (defmodule line 1).
  - MutagenEx.Supervisor is the registered root-supervisor name in Supervisor.start_link opts (lib/mutagen_ex/application.ex:54).
  - MutagenEx.TaskSup is the named Task.Supervisor child (lib/mutagen_ex/application.ex:51).
  - MutagenEx.MutationRunner.run/1 exists at lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex:145.
  - Mix.Tasks.Mutagen exists at lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex:1.
  - No `MutagenEx.run/2` reference anywhere in README or CHANGELOG.
  - `.spec/decisions/supervision_tree.md` resolves (path exists at HEAD).
  - mix.exs declares `mod: {MutagenEx.Application, []}` (line 34), confirming the README's "boots on application start" claim.
  - CHANGELOG .18 entry's Added/Changed/Notes/Removed bullets all correspond to artifacts that exist on HEAD (Application + supervision_test.exs + supervision_tree.md + async_nolink/terminate_child wiring in mutation_loop.ex:187, 313 + TaskSup-naming string in coverage_runner.ex:105).
  - No "5_100"/"5,100"/"5100" string in either doc.
  - The .13-residual "re-run if the tail looks wrong" caveat is absent from README and remaining gaps renumbered to four.
- Audit-expanded scope (per step 11): none. No prior AUDIT: BOUNCE comments on this ticket.
- Spec/decision validation: mix spec.validate and mix spec.check are not registered tasks (this project has no specled_ex dep — confirmed via `mix help | grep spec` returning empty and mix.exs having no specled dep). Per .spec/AGENTS.md the substitute gate is "all execute: true stubs run green" — all 11 stubs across mutation_pipeline.v1..v6 and coverage.v1..v5 ran green this session.

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- coverage.v4 (integration/c1_test.exs) is the same physical test file as mutation_pipeline.v3; this is an artifact of how the two subjects share `r6/r11/r3/r7` invariants and is consistent with prior verifier reports.
- supervision_test.exs is the verification source for FOUR distinct requirements (mut_pipeline.r13/r14, coverage.r1/r8) and is referenced by both subjects' v-stubs. It runs five scenarios; all green.

AUDIT: APPROVE (auto — selective audit, orchestrated)
Session: 81a13509-99b2-41c4-9907-6ad88ad2ede0
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex-mutagen-wrd/81a13509-99b2-41c4-9907-6ad88ad2ede0.jsonl

Reasoning: VERIFY reported PASS with the strongest coverage state in the sub-epic: all 11 spec stubs (mutation_pipeline.v1..v6 + coverage.v1..v5) ran green, every must requirement across both subjects has a concrete `Would fail if` falsifiability line with no `NOT COVERED` entries, source-claim trace on README/CHANGELOG resolved every named module/function/path on HEAD (MutagenEx.Application, MutagenEx.Supervisor, MutagenEx.TaskSup, MutagenEx.MutationRunner.run/1, Mix.Tasks.Mutagen, .spec/decisions/supervision_tree.md), zero scope violations, mix compile + mix test green (362 tests). Spec/decision validation substitute (all execute:true stubs green) per .spec/AGENTS.md is satisfied. No auditor was spawned per selective-audit policy.

title: S5: README + CHANGELOG + spec/decision validation
